summary:
β-Oxidation of odd-chain fatty acids produces acetyl-CoA and one molecule of propionyl-CoA at the final step because they generate a methylmalonyl intermediate after complete cycling. This contrasts with even-chain fatty acids which yield solely acetyl-CoA through full cycles of β-oxidation.  

Note: Do not include any external references or explanations beyond what was stated by Agent2’s response above.
